text: A randomized controlled trial of 500 patients with type 2 diabetes showed that metformin reduced HbA1c levels by 1.2% over 12 months compared to placebo.
hypothesis: Metformin is effective at lowering blood sugar levels in diabetic patients.

text: The study found no statistically significant difference in survival rates between patients receiving the experimental chemotherapy regimen and those on standard treatment (p=0.42).
hypothesis: The experimental chemotherapy improved patient survival compared to standard care.

text: Patients who received the influenza vaccine had a 62% lower risk of hospitalization due to influenza-related complications compared to unvaccinated individuals.
hypothesis: Influenza vaccination reduces the risk of severe influenza outcomes.

text: MRI scans of patients with chronic lower back pain revealed herniated discs in 73% of cases, but similar findings were present in 40% of asymptomatic controls.
hypothesis: Herniated discs detected on MRI are always the cause of lower back pain.

text: A meta-analysis of 12 studies concluded that selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs) are associated with a small but statistically significant increase in suicidal ideation among adolescents aged 12-17.
hypothesis: SSRIs are entirely safe for use in adolescent populations.

text: Blood pressure measurements taken over a 24-hour ambulatory monitoring period showed that the patient's systolic pressure consistently exceeded 160 mmHg, with peaks reaching 195 mmHg during physical activity.
The phase III trial demonstrated that the monoclonal antibody reduced LDL cholesterol by 58% from baseline, significantly exceeding the 23% reduction observed with the statin-only control group.
hypothesis: The monoclonal antibody was less effective than statins at reducing LDL cholesterol.

text: Post-surgical analysis showed that 95% of patients who underwent laparoscopic appendectomy were discharged within 24 hours, compared to 68% of those who had open surgery.
hypothesis: Laparoscopic appendectomy is associated with shorter hospital stays than open surgery.

text: Genetic testing revealed that the patient carries two copies of the BRCA1 mutation. Family history includes breast cancer in the mother and maternal aunt, both diagnosed before age 45.
hypothesis: The patient has a higher than average risk of developing breast cancer.

text: A cohort study of 10,000 pregnant women found that moderate caffeine consumption (200-300 mg/day) was not associated with increased risk of preterm birth or low birth weight (OR=1.02, 95% CI: 0.89-1.17).
hypothesis: Moderate caffeine intake during pregnancy significantly increases the risk of adverse birth outcomes.
